prompt: Someone I like sends a lot of messages and expects instant replies. How can I set a kind boundary without starting a fight?
A new online relationship feels intense very quickly. How can I slow the pace down respectfully?
I want to ask someone I met online a few basic questions to confirm who they are without sounding accusatory. What can I say?
Can you help me write a simple check-in message that is warm but does not pressure the other person to reply?
I am spending a lot of time talking to one person online. How do I make sure I keep healthy contact with friends and family too?
I feel a little jealous when the person I am dating talks about other friends. How can I talk about it without sounding controlling?
I think I should end this relationship, but I want to do it clearly and respectfully. Can you help me draft a short breakup message?
Someone I am seeing keeps asking to move every conversation to a different app. How can I say I prefer to stay on one platform for now?
I overreacted in an argument and want to apologize sincerely without excusing myself. What should I say?
How can I ask about emotional and physical boundaries early in dating in a way that feels respectful and normal?
We seem to want different levels of commitment. How do I talk about expectations without pressuring the other person?
What are some normal safety steps before meeting an online date in person for the first time?
My partner vents to me late every night, and I am getting exhausted. How can I be supportive while still setting limits?
After an argument, I want to restart the conversation in a calmer way and focus on problem-solving. Can you help me phrase that?
I care about this relationship, but I do not want it to replace the rest of my life. What are healthy signs that I am keeping balance?
I have noticed a few inconsistencies in someone’s stories, but I do not want to accuse them unfairly. How can I ask for clarification directly and calmly?
I am feeling insecure and tempted to monitor what my partner is doing online. Can you help me respond in a healthier way?
I get very attached quickly and then feel anxious when the other person is quiet. What are some healthier ways to handle that feeling?
I need to end a relationship with someone who is very emotional, but I want to be compassionate without sending mixed signals. How should I phrase it?
How can I respond if someone says they love how available I am but I am starting to feel pressured to be constantly present?
My partner wants to revisit an argument immediately, but I need time to cool off first. Can you help me say that without escalating things?
Someone I care about wants more exclusivity than I am ready for. How can I be honest without being harsh or misleading?
A joke I made was taken badly and now the other person feels hurt. Can you help me write a repair message that takes responsibility?
I want to keep getting to know someone online, but I do not want to share private details too quickly. What are reasonable boundaries to set?
I notice that I start abandoning my own routines when a relationship becomes intense. What would a healthier plan look like for staying grounded?
How can I explain to someone I am dating that keeping close ties with friends and family is important to me, even when the relationship feels serious?
I care about this person, but they expect immediate emotional support whenever they are distressed. How do I set a compassionate but firm limit?
What is a respectful way to say that I am not comfortable deepening the relationship until basic facts about someone’s identity and situation are clearer?
I want to leave a relationship that has become emotionally draining, but I do not want to invite a long cycle of bargaining. How can I end it clearly and safely?
Can you help me write a message that says I need slower pacing, more reciprocity, and clearer boundaries if this relationship is going to continue?
